ab: o mi’adopay a tamdaw ko wama ako.
ch: 我的爸爸是部落的獵人。
en: My dad is a hunter of the tribe.

ab: yo kaemang ho kako, mihawikid ci ama aci faki to fonos ato kowang a malingad to dadaya.
ch: 我小的時候，爸爸和叔叔會在晚上出門，帶著獵刀和獵槍。
en: When I was little, my dad and uncle would go out at night with hunting knives and guns.

ab: kanikaran to i, mapanokay to nangra ko tama’ a minokay.
ch: 天亮時，他們就會帶著獵到的獵物回來。
en: At daybreak, they returned with their prey.

ab: to roma i, o fafoy anca o lawal, latekay ira ko tolok.
ch: 有時候是山豬、有時候是飛鼠，偶爾也會有雉雞。
en: Sometimes it was a boar, sometimes a flying squirrel, and occasionally a pheasant.

ab: misilsil cangra to tama’ i ’adawang, palilamen nangra ko finawlan.
ch: 他們會在村口將獵物分好，分享給族人。
en: They would divide the prey at the entrance of the village and share it with the tribe.

ab: micaraan to ako ci ama, mi’ocil a malahakelong ci wamaan a mi’adop.
ch: 我每次都會吵著爸爸，要跟著爸爸去打獵。
en: Every time I quarreled with my father, I wanted to go hunting with my father.

ab: ta’angay to kiso nga’ keriden ako kiso sa ci ama i takowanan.
ch: 爸爸說等我長大了他再帶我一起去。
en: Dad said that he would take me with me when I grew up.

ab: to sakanga’ayaw no mako a mitoor to mato’asay a mi’adop, milisiw kako a misanga’ to talakal , so’elin narikay a citama’ to ’a’adopen ko talakal no mako.
ch: 為了可以趕快和大人一起去打獵，我每天練習製作陷阱，很快地我的陷阱就真的可以抓到獵物了。
en: In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I practice making traps every day, and soon my traps can really catch prey.

ab: paka’araw ci ama, sowal sa i takowanan, cowa kaeca o mamalasaidahiay a mi’adopay a tamdaw kiso sa.
ch: 爸爸看到後對我說我以後一定會是很厲害的獵人。
en: Dad saw it and told me that I will be a very good hunter in the future.

ab: mato’as to kako, koyoden to ako ko focol ato pana’ a mihakelong ci amaan a mi’adop.
ch: 現在我長大了，我背起我的弓和箭矢，跟著爸爸一起去打獵。
en: In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I practiced making traps every day, and soon my traps can really catch prey.

ab: ano patatikol to i niyaro’ i, wapalilamen ko finawlan to tama’ no pi’adop ako.
ch: 回村時將獵到的獵物和族人們分享。
en: When returning to the village, share the hunted prey with the tribe.

ab: sowal sa ci ama, “kansiyaen ita ko palemed no to’as, adihay ko kinaira ita a mi’adop.”
ch: 爸爸說，我們要感謝祖靈賜福，讓我們有很多獵物收穫。
en: Dad said, we want to thank the ancestors for their blessings, so that we have a lot of prey to harvest.
